page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, has no local storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and features a 480 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits strong single-core performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench text processing and strong ratings in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling presents a tradeoff, with stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the lower 25th percentile.

Although classified as a memory-optimized instance with high-speed DDR5 RAM, memory bandwidth benchmarks show mixed results, with large-block write performance in the average tier but read-only bandwidth falling into the bottom 25th percentile. The instance lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, making it reliant on network-attached storage. Given these performance characteristics, the r8i-flex.xlarge is suited for memory-intensive workloads that rely on strong single-threaded execution, such as caching layers, text processing pipelines, and moderate database operations, while being less optimal for highly parallelized multi-core computing or heavy local storage I/O.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores, running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, though multi-core scaling and read-only memory bandwidth fall into lower percentiles. With advanced instruction support like Intel AMX and AVX-512, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-demanding tasks that prioritize single-threaded performance over massive parallelization, such as in-memory caching, text processing, and lightweight database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and strong single-core Intel Xeon performance for memory-intensive applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8 GB per vCPU)
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage
Best suited for in-memory caching, text processing, and database operations
